CHANGE RECORD 
When the quality of the products change s, the QuID is updated and a row is added to this table.  The 
third column specifies which sections  or sub-sections have been updated.   The fourth column should 
mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

1.0 31/01/2019 All Creation of the document Clément Bricaud   
 
 
 
 
Product name: GLOBAL_OMI_WMHE_heattrp 
Dataset name: global_omi_wmhe_heattrp 
 
Indicator evaluation method:  
Heat transports across lines are define d by the integration of heat  fluxes along some sections 
and from top to bottom of the Ocean: 
, 
 
𝐻𝐸𝐴𝑇𝑇𝑅𝑃 =  𝜌 ∗  𝐶𝑝 ∗ ∫ ∫ 𝑇 ∗ 𝑣 ∗ 𝑆 𝑑𝑥 𝑑𝑧
𝐵
𝐴
𝑧=𝑏𝑜𝑡𝑡𝑜𝑚
𝑧=0 , 
where: 
A and B are the 2 extremities of the section, 
v is the horizontal ocean velocity, 
T is the ocean temperature, 
and S in the surface of the section, 
𝜌 is the reference Ocean density ( 1026 kg.m-3), 
and  𝐶𝑝 is the Ocean specific heat ( 4000 J kg-1 K-1) 
They are computed with reanalysis daily outputs and given in p etaWatt (PW, 1 PW=1.e15 
Watt).

They are computed with reanalysis daily outputs. 
 
Scientific validation: 
More details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report  
(von Schuckmann et al., 2016; von Schuckmann et al., 2018). 
The quality evaluation of GLOBAL_OMI_WMHE_heattrp is based on the “multi -product” 
approach as introduced in the second issue of the Ocean State Report (CMEMS OSR, 2017).  
The transports are computed from an ensemble of 4 CMEMS Global Ocean reanalysis at ¼°: 
GLORYS2V4 from Mercator Ocean, ORAS5 from ECMWF, GloSea5 from Met Office and C-
GLORS05 from CMCC. 
Some previous estimations from Lumpkin and Speer (2007) are proposed in the plot. 
 
Figure 1:  HEat transport (units PW ) from the multi -product approach averaged over the period 1993 -2014 and 
the 2017 (both red). Estimates of Lumpkin and Speer (2007) have been added for comparison (blue). Uncertainty 
ranges are derived from the ensemble standard deviation. Arrows indicate the direction of the mean flow through 
the sections.

Product used for indicator evaluation: 
The transports are computed from an ensemble of 4 CMEMS different Global Ocean reanalysis 
at ¼° (GLOBAL_REANALYSIS_PHY_001_02): GLORYS2V4 from Mercator Ocean, 
ORAS5 from ECMWF, GloSea5 from Met Office and C-GLORS05 from CMCC. They are 
based on NEMO OGCM (Madec, 2008), and using a data assimilation system. 
 
References: 
Lumpkin R, Speer K. 2007. Global ocean meridional overturning. J. Phys. Oceanogr., 37, 
2550–2562, doi:10.1175/JPO3130.1. 
Madec G : NEMO ocean engine, Note du Pôle de modélisation, Institut Pierre-Simon Laplace (IPSL), 
France, No 27, ISSN No 1288-1619, 2008 
Bricaud C, Drillet Y, Garric G. 2016. Ocean mass and heat transport. In CMEMS Ocean State Report, Journal of 
Operational Oceanography, 9, http://dx.doi.org/10.1080/1755876X.2016.1273446
